title: [InstCombine] Reland #165975: Fix #163110: Support peeling off matching shifts from icmp operands via canEvaluateShifted
author: Michael-Chen-NJU
description:
This relanding of #165975 fixes the bug that caused the bootstrap-asan buildbot failure (https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/#/builders/52/builds/16329).

## Original optimization

Consider a pattern like: `icmp (shl nsw/nuw X, L), (add nsw/nuw (shl nsw/nuw Y, L), K)`

When K is a multiple of 2^L, this can be simplified to: `icmp X, (add nsw/nuw Y, K >> L)`

This patch extends `canEvaluateShifted` to support `Instruction::Add` and refactors its signature to accept a `ShiftSemantics` enum (`Lossy` / `Unsigned` / `Signed`) instead of a bare opcode. This allows the function to enforce losslessness requirements according to the overflow flags (nsw/nuw) of the operands. The logic is wired into `foldICmpCommutative` to fold `icmp (shl nuw/nsw X, C), Op1` into `icmp X, (Op1 >> C)` when Op1 can be right-shifted losslessly by C bits.

## Bug fixed since #165975

`canEvaluateShiftedShift` unconditionally returned `true` for same-direction logical shifts, ignoring the `ShiftSemantics` parameter. This caused `foldICmpCommutative` to incorrectly transform:

  `icmp uge (shl nuw X, 4), (lshr Y, 1)  →  icmp uge X, (lshr Y, 5)  ← WRONG`

**Fix:** `canEvaluateShiftedShift` now returns `true` for same-direction shifts only when `Semantics == ShiftSemantics::Lossy`, blocking the unsafe fold for `Unsigned`/`Signed` semantics. Regression test added in `Transforms/InstCombine/icmp_shl_lshr_fold.ll`.

tests.test_body:
define i37 @test25(i37 %AA, i37 %BB) {
  %C = lshr i37 %BB, 17
  %D = lshr i37 %AA, 17
  %E = add i37 %D, %C
  %F = shl i37 %E, 17
  ret i37 %F
}

define i1 @shl_nuw_ne(i8 %a, i8 %b, i8 %c, i1 %cond) {
  %shl_a = shl nuw i8 %a, 3
  %shl_b = shl nuw i8 %b, 3
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i8 %shl_b, i8 32
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %sel, %shl_a
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_nuw_ugt(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nuw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nuw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nuw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_no_nsw_on_v1(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 32
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

declare void @use_i64(i64)

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_multi_use(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  call void @use_i64(i64 %v1)
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 32
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_var_amount_nuw(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nuw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nuw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nuw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_nuw_ult(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nuw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nuw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nuw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_i8(i8 %v0, i8 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i8 %v0, 3
  %v4 = shl nsw i8 %v3, 3
  %v5 = add nsw i8 %v4, 8
  %v6 = icmp eq i8 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define <2 x i1> @shl_add_const_eq_vec_non_splat(<2 x i64> %v0, <2 x i64> %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v0, <i64 5, i64 6>
  %v4 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v3, <i64 5, i64 6>
  %v5 = add nsw <2 x i64> %v4, <i64 32, i64 64>
  %v6 = icmp eq <2 x i64> %v1, %v5
  ret <2 x i1> %v6
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_i32(i32 %v0, i32 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i32 %v0, 10
  %v4 = shl nsw i32 %v3, 10
  %v5 = add nsw i32 %v4, 1024
  %v6 = icmp eq i32 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_mismatch_shl_amt(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 4
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 16
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_k_multiple_of_pow2(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 64
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_nsw_slt(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nsw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nsw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define <2 x i1> @shl_add_const_eq_vec_splat(<2 x i64> %v0, <2 x i64> %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v0, splat (i64 5)
  %v4 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v3, splat (i64 5)
  %v5 = add nsw <2 x i64> %v4, splat (i64 32)
  %v6 = icmp eq <2 x i64> %v1, %v5
  ret <2 x i1> %v6
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_base(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 32
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_eq_commutative(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 32
  %v6 = icmp eq i64 %v5, %v1
  ret i1 %v6
}

define <2 x i1> @shl_add_const_eq_vec_splat_poison(<2 x i64> %v0, <2 x i64> %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v0, splat (i64 5)
  %v4 = shl nsw <2 x i64> %v3, splat (i64 5)
  %v5 = add nsw <2 x i64> %v4, <i64 32, i64 poison>
  %v6 = icmp eq <2 x i64> %v1, %v5
  ret <2 x i1> %v6
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_nsw_sgt(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nsw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nsw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_ugt_shl_nuw_add_nsw_fuzz(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nuw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nsw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @shl_add_const_ne(i64 %v0, i64 %v3) {
  %v1 = shl nsw i64 %v0, 5
  %v4 = shl nsw i64 %v3, 5
  %v5 = add nsw i64 %v4, 32
  %v6 = icmp ne i64 %v1, %v5
  ret i1 %v6
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_var_amount_nsw(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nsw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nsw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_shl_nsw_sle(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nsw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nsw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 %shly, 32
  %cmp = icmp sle i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_slt_shl_nsw_add_nsw_fuzz(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %shlx = shl nsw i32 %x, 5
  %shly = shl nuw i32 %y, 5
  %add = add nsw i32 32, %shly
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %shlx, %add
  ret i1 %cmp
}
